title: QSGW self-consistency cycle
summary: The iterative loop in quasiparticle self-consistent GW (QSGW) where the screened Coulomb interaction W and the self-energy Sigma are updated from the current Green's function G until the quasiparticle energies converge. This cycle is the core computational workflow of the first-principles (code-method) lane.
assumptions: A mean-field starting point (e.g., LDA or PBE) provides the initial Green's function.
Basis-set convergence can be monitored by tracking quasiparticle energy drift across iterations.

approximations: static or quasiparticle approximation to Sigma
boundary_conditions: convergence criterion on total energy or band gap change between iterations
fixed crystal structure throughout the QSGW cycle

applies_to: code-method lane validation: QSGW convergence tracking and benchmark comparison

citations: Faleev, van Schilfgaarde, Kotani 2004, Phys. Rev. Lett. 93, 126406
van Schilfgaarde, Kotani, Faleev 2006, Phys. Rev. Lett. 96, 226402

promotion.rationale: Seed unit for first-principles (code-method) lane: QSGW self-consistency cycle as the core computational workflow concept.

payload.definition: The QSGW self-consistency cycle iterates: (1) compute polarizability P from G, (2) compute screened interaction W = v + vPW, (3) compute self-energy Sigma = iGW, (4) construct quasiparticle Hamiltonian H_QP, (5) diagonalize to obtain new G. Repeat until H_QP energies converge.
payload.operational_role: Core iteration loop for code-method lane validation; convergence behavior (iteration count, energy drift, AC sensitivity) is the primary audit target.
